codeExamples.title: Cloud Storage Example (AWS S3)

python.code:
import boto3

# Create S3 client
s3 = boto3.client('s3')

# Upload a file to cloud storage
def upload_to_cloud(file_name, bucket, object_name=None):
    if object_name is None:
        object_name = file_name
    
    try:
        s3.upload_file(file_name, bucket, object_name)
        print(f"File {file_name} uploaded to {bucket}/{object_name}")
        return True
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error: {e}")
        return False

# Download from cloud
def download_from_cloud(bucket, object_name, file_name):
    s3.download_file(bucket, object_name, file_name)
    print(f"Downloaded {object_name} to {file_name}")

# Usage
upload_to_cloud('local_file.txt', 'my-bucket')
download_from_cloud('my-bucket', 'local_file.txt', 'downloaded.txt')

bash.code:
#!/bin/bash
# AWS CLI examples for cloud storage

# Configure AWS CLI
aws configure

# Create a bucket (storage container in cloud)
aws s3 mb s3://my-unique-bucket-name

# Upload file to cloud
aws s3 cp local_file.txt s3://my-bucket/

# List files in bucket
aws s3 ls s3://my-bucket/

# Download from cloud
aws s3 cp s3://my-bucket/remote_file.txt ./local_copy.txt

# Sync entire directory
aws s3 sync ./local_folder s3://my-bucket/folder/

# Delete file from cloud
aws s3 rm s3://my-bucket/file.txt

echo "Cloud operations complete!"

codeExamples.description: Basic cloud storage operations demonstrating IaaS usage
